commands.name: curl
commands.description: Transfer data to or from a server using various protocols

patterns.text: download a file from a URL
patterns.explanation: Downloads a file preserving the remote filename

patterns.text: make a POST request with JSON data
patterns.explanation: Sends a JSON payload via POST to an API endpoint

patterns.text: follow redirects and show response headers
patterns.explanation: Follows redirects (-L) and displays only response headers (-I)

patterns.text: send a request with a bearer token for authentication
patterns.explanation: Includes an authorization header with a bearer token

patterns.text: upload a file using multipart form data
patterns.explanation: Uploads a file as multipart/form-data to the server

commands.name: wget
commands.description: Non-interactive network downloader for retrieving files via HTTP, HTTPS, and FTP

patterns.text: download a file in the background
patterns.explanation: Downloads a file in the background, logging progress to wget-log

patterns.text: mirror an entire website for offline browsing
patterns.explanation: Recursively downloads an entire site with links converted for local viewing

patterns.text: resume a partially downloaded file
patterns.explanation: Continues a previously interrupted download from where it left off

patterns.text: download a list of URLs from a file
patterns.explanation: Reads URLs from a text file and downloads each one

patterns.text: limit download speed to avoid saturating bandwidth
patterns.explanation: Restricts download speed to 500 kilobytes per second

commands.name: ssh
commands.description: Securely connect to a remote machine over an encrypted connection

patterns.text: connect to a remote server as a specific user
patterns.explanation: Opens an SSH session to the remote host as the given user

patterns.text: set up a local port forward to access a remote service
patterns.explanation: Forwards local port 8080 to port 80 on the remote server

patterns.text: run a command on a remote server without opening a shell
patterns.explanation: Executes the command remotely and returns output to the local terminal

patterns.text: connect using a specific private key file
patterns.explanation: Authenticates using the specified identity file instead of the default key

patterns.text: create a SOCKS proxy tunnel through a remote server
patterns.explanation: Sets up a dynamic SOCKS proxy on local port 9090 tunneled through the remote server

patterns.text: jump through a bastion host to reach a private server
patterns.explanation: ProxyJump opens the second hop through the first without manual netcat hacks

commands.name: ssh-keygen
commands.description: Generate, convert, and manage SSH authentication keys

patterns.text: create a new ed25519 key with a comment
patterns.explanation: Ed25519 keys are short, fast, and recommended for new deployments

patterns.text: remove passphrase from a key non-interactively for automation
patterns.explanation: Use only for throwaway CI keys; empty passphrases weaken security

patterns.text: print the fingerprint of a public key to compare with a server
patterns.explanation: Shows MD5 or SHA256 fingerprint depending on OpenSSH version

commands.name: ssh-add
commands.description: Add private keys to the ssh-agent for passwordless use in a session

patterns.text: load the default identities into the agent
patterns.explanation: Prompts for passphrases if needed and caches unlocked keys

patterns.text: add a specific key file
patterns.explanation: Registers that key for subsequent ssh and git operations

patterns.text: list keys currently loaded in the agent
patterns.explanation: Shows fingerprints of each loaded key

commands.name: scp
commands.description: Securely copy files between local and remote hosts over SSH

patterns.text: copy a file from local machine to a remote server
patterns.explanation: Uploads a local file to the specified path on the remote server

patterns.text: download a file from a remote server to the local machine
patterns.explanation: Copies a file from the remote server to a local directory

patterns.text: copy an entire directory recursively to a remote server
patterns.explanation: Recursively copies the directory and its contents to the remote host

patterns.text: copy a file between two remote servers
patterns.explanation: Transfers a file directly between two remote hosts

patterns.text: copy a file using a specific SSH port
patterns.explanation: Uses a non-default SSH port for the transfer

commands.name: sftp
commands.description: Interactive secure file transfer program over SSH

patterns.text: start an interactive SFTP session with a remote server
patterns.explanation: Opens an interactive SFTP session for browsing and transferring files

patterns.text: download a remote file directly without interactive mode
patterns.explanation: Fetches a specific remote file to a local directory

patterns.text: use a batch file to automate SFTP transfers
patterns.explanation: Executes SFTP commands from a batch file non-interactively

patterns.text: connect to SFTP on a non-standard port
patterns.explanation: Connects to the SFTP server on a custom SSH port

commands.name: rsync
commands.description: Fast and versatile file synchronization tool for local and remote transfers

patterns.text: sync a local directory to a remote server with progress
patterns.explanation: Archives, compresses, and syncs files showing transfer progress

patterns.text: sync files and delete those that no longer exist in source
patterns.explanation: Mirrors source to destination, removing extra files at the destination

patterns.text: do a dry run to see what would be transferred without making changes
patterns.explanation: Simulates the sync and shows what would happen without actually copying

patterns.text: sync files excluding certain patterns like log files
patterns.explanation: Syncs files while skipping log files and git directories

patterns.text: resume an interrupted transfer with partial files
patterns.explanation: Keeps partially transferred files so the sync can resume later

commands.name: ping
commands.description: Send ICMP echo requests to test network connectivity to a host

patterns.text: check if a server is reachable
patterns.explanation: Sends 4 ICMP echo requests to test connectivity to google.com

patterns.text: ping a host continuously until interrupted
patterns.explanation: Continuously pings the host until you press Ctrl+C

patterns.text: ping with a specific packet size to test MTU
patterns.explanation: Sends pings with a specific payload size to test maximum transmission unit

patterns.text: ping with a short interval for quick connectivity testing
patterns.explanation: Pings every 0.2 seconds for faster results

patterns.text: ping only using IPv6
patterns.explanation: Sends ICMPv6 echo requests to test IPv6 connectivity

commands.name: traceroute
commands.description: Trace the route packets take to reach a network host

patterns.text: trace the network path to a remote host
patterns.explanation: Shows each router hop between your machine and the destination

patterns.text: trace route using TCP instead of UDP to bypass firewalls
patterns.explanation: Uses TCP SYN packets which are more likely to pass through firewalls

patterns.text: trace route without resolving hostnames for faster output
patterns.explanation: Shows only IP addresses instead of resolving DNS names at each hop

patterns.text: limit the number of hops to trace
patterns.explanation: Sets the maximum number of hops (TTL) to 15

commands.name: dig
commands.description: DNS lookup utility for querying name servers

patterns.text: look up the IP address of a domain
patterns.explanation: Queries DNS for A records of the domain

patterns.text: find the mail servers for a domain
patterns.explanation: Queries for MX (mail exchange) records

patterns.text: get just the answer without extra info
patterns.explanation: Returns only the IP address without verbose DNS information

patterns.text: query a specific DNS server for a domain
patterns.explanation: Uses Google's DNS server to resolve the domain

patterns.text: do a reverse DNS lookup from an IP address
patterns.explanation: Finds the hostname associated with the given IP address

commands.name: nslookup
commands.description: Query internet name servers interactively or non-interactively

patterns.text: look up the DNS records for a domain
patterns.explanation: Queries the default DNS server for the domain's address

patterns.text: query a specific DNS server for a domain
patterns.explanation: Uses a specified DNS server to resolve the domain

patterns.text: find the name servers for a domain
patterns.explanation: Queries for NS records to find authoritative name servers

patterns.text: do a reverse DNS lookup on an IP address
patterns.explanation: Finds the hostname for the given IP address via reverse DNS

commands.name: host
commands.description: Simple DNS lookup utility

patterns.text: quickly find the IP address of a domain
patterns.explanation: Performs a simple DNS lookup and shows the IP address

patterns.text: find all DNS record types for a domain
patterns.explanation: Queries for all record types including A, AAAA, MX, NS, and TXT

patterns.text: look up the TXT records for a domain
patterns.explanation: Queries specifically for TXT records, useful for SPF and DKIM

patterns.text: do a reverse lookup from IP to hostname
patterns.explanation: Performs a reverse DNS query on the IP address

commands.name: nc
commands.description: Netcat - versatile networking utility for reading and writing across network connections

patterns.text: check if a specific port is open on a remote host
patterns.explanation: Tests connectivity to port 443 without sending data

patterns.text: scan a range of ports on a host
patterns.explanation: Scans ports 20 through 100 to see which are open

patterns.text: set up a simple TCP listener on a port
patterns.explanation: Listens on port 8080 for incoming connections

patterns.text: transfer a file over the network using netcat
patterns.explanation: Listens on port 9999 and saves incoming data to a file

patterns.text: send a file to a listening netcat instance
patterns.explanation: Sends the contents of file.txt to the target host on port 9999

commands.name: ss
commands.description: Utility to investigate sockets and display network connections

patterns.text: show all listening TCP ports
patterns.explanation: Lists all TCP listening sockets with port numbers and process info

patterns.text: show all established connections
patterns.explanation: Displays only currently established TCP connections

patterns.text: find which process is using a specific port
patterns.explanation: Filters listening sockets to find what is using port 8080

patterns.text: show summary statistics of all socket types
patterns.explanation: Displays a summary of socket counts by type and state

patterns.text: list all UDP sockets with process information
patterns.explanation: Shows all UDP listening sockets with associated processes

commands.name: netstat
commands.description: Display network connections, routing tables, and interface statistics

patterns.text: show all active network connections and listening ports
patterns.explanation: Lists all TCP and UDP connections with port numbers and process IDs

patterns.text: display the routing table
patterns.explanation: Shows the kernel routing table with numeric addresses

patterns.text: show network interface statistics
patterns.explanation: Displays statistics for all network interfaces

patterns.text: show only listening sockets
patterns.explanation: Lists all sockets in the listening state

patterns.text: continuously display network connections every 2 seconds
patterns.explanation: Refreshes and displays connection info continuously

commands.name: ip
commands.description: Show and manipulate routing, network devices, interfaces, and tunnels

patterns.text: show all IP addresses assigned to network interfaces
patterns.explanation: Displays IP addresses for all network interfaces

patterns.text: bring a network interface up or down
patterns.explanation: Enables the eth0 network interface

patterns.text: add a static IP address to an interface
patterns.explanation: Assigns the IP 192.168.1.50 with a /24 subnet to eth0

patterns.text: show the routing table
patterns.explanation: Displays all routes in the kernel routing table

patterns.text: add a default gateway
patterns.explanation: Sets the default gateway to 192.168.1.1

commands.name: ifconfig
commands.description: Configure or display network interface parameters (legacy tool)

patterns.text: show all network interfaces and their IP addresses
patterns.explanation: Displays configuration for all active network interfaces

patterns.text: show details for a specific network interface
patterns.explanation: Shows IP address, MAC address, and stats for eth0

patterns.text: assign an IP address to an interface
patterns.explanation: Sets the IP and subnet mask on the specified interface

patterns.text: disable a network interface
patterns.explanation: Brings the eth0 interface down

commands.name: arp
commands.description: Manipulate or display the system ARP cache

patterns.text: show the ARP table of known MAC addresses on the network
patterns.explanation: Displays all entries in the ARP cache with hostnames

patterns.text: display the ARP table with numeric addresses only
patterns.explanation: Shows ARP entries without resolving hostnames

patterns.text: delete an entry from the ARP cache
patterns.explanation: Removes the ARP entry for the specified IP address

patterns.text: add a static ARP entry
patterns.explanation: Manually maps an IP address to a MAC address in the ARP table

commands.name: route
commands.description: Show or manipulate the IP routing table

patterns.text: display the current routing table
patterns.explanation: Shows the kernel routing table with numeric addresses

patterns.text: add a default gateway
patterns.explanation: Sets the default gateway for outbound traffic

patterns.text: add a route to a specific network via a gateway
patterns.explanation: Routes traffic for the 10.0.0.0/8 network through the specified gateway

patterns.text: delete a route from the routing table
patterns.explanation: Removes the route entry for the specified network

commands.name: nmap
commands.description: Network exploration tool and security scanner

patterns.text: scan a host for open ports
patterns.explanation: Performs a TCP SYN scan of the most common 1000 ports

patterns.text: detect what operating system a remote host is running
patterns.explanation: Uses TCP/IP fingerprinting to identify the OS

patterns.text: scan an entire subnet to discover live hosts
patterns.explanation: Performs a ping scan to find which hosts are up without port scanning

patterns.text: detect service versions running on open ports
patterns.explanation: Probes open ports to determine the service and version running

patterns.text: run a comprehensive scan with OS detection, scripts, and version info
patterns.explanation: Enables OS detection, version scanning, script scanning, and traceroute

patterns.text: scan specific ports on a target
patterns.explanation: Scans only the specified ports on the target host

commands.name: tcpdump
commands.description: Capture and analyze network traffic on an interface

patterns.text: capture all traffic on a specific network interface
patterns.explanation: Captures and displays all packets on the eth0 interface

patterns.text: capture traffic to or from a specific host
patterns.explanation: Filters packets involving the specified IP address

patterns.text: capture only HTTP traffic on port 80
patterns.explanation: Captures packets on port 80 across all interfaces

patterns.text: save captured packets to a file for later analysis
patterns.explanation: Writes raw packets to a pcap file that can be opened in Wireshark

patterns.text: capture DNS traffic to debug name resolution
patterns.explanation: Captures all DNS query and response packets

commands.name: iptables
commands.description: Administration tool for IPv4 packet filtering and NAT

patterns.text: list all current firewall rules
patterns.explanation: Displays all rules with numeric addresses and packet counters

patterns.text: block all incoming traffic from a specific IP
patterns.explanation: Appends a rule to drop all packets from the given source IP

patterns.text: allow incoming SSH connections on port 22
patterns.explanation: Permits inbound TCP traffic on port 22

patterns.text: flush all firewall rules to start fresh
patterns.explanation: Removes all rules from all chains

patterns.text: set up NAT masquerading for internet sharing
patterns.explanation: Enables NAT on outgoing interface for routing traffic from internal network

commands.name: ufw
commands.description: Uncomplicated Firewall - simplified interface for managing firewall rules

patterns.text: enable the firewall with default deny incoming
patterns.explanation: Activates the firewall; default policy denies incoming traffic

patterns.text: allow SSH connections through the firewall
patterns.explanation: Opens port 22 for incoming TCP connections

patterns.text: show current firewall rules and status
patterns.explanation: Displays active rules, default policies, and firewall state

patterns.text: allow traffic from a specific IP address only
patterns.explanation: Permits all connections from the specified IP

patterns.text: deny incoming traffic on a specific port
patterns.explanation: Blocks incoming TCP connections on port 3306 (MySQL)

commands.name: lsof
commands.description: List open files and network connections associated with processes

patterns.text: find which process is listening on a specific port
patterns.explanation: Shows the process bound to port 8080

patterns.text: show all network connections for a specific process
patterns.explanation: Lists all network connections opened by process ID 1234

patterns.text: list all established TCP connections
patterns.explanation: Shows all currently established TCP connections

patterns.text: find all processes listening on any port
patterns.explanation: Lists all listening network sockets with numeric ports and addresses

patterns.text: show all open network connections on the system
patterns.explanation: Lists all open Internet sockets

commands.name: mtr
commands.description: Network diagnostic tool combining ping and traceroute functionality

patterns.text: run a continuous traceroute with live latency stats
patterns.explanation: Displays a continuously updating traceroute with packet loss and latency

patterns.text: generate a text report of network path quality
patterns.explanation: Sends 100 probes and outputs a summary report of the route

patterns.text: run mtr without resolving hostnames for faster output
patterns.explanation: Shows only IP addresses at each hop without DNS resolution

patterns.text: use TCP mode for mtr to test through strict firewalls
patterns.explanation: Uses TCP on port 443 instead of ICMP for firewall-friendly probing

commands.name: whois
commands.description: Query domain registration and IP address allocation information

patterns.text: look up domain registration information
patterns.explanation: Shows registrar, creation date, expiration, and contact info for the domain

patterns.text: find out who owns an IP address
patterns.explanation: Queries the IP allocation registry for ownership information

patterns.text: check when a domain expires
patterns.explanation: Filters whois output to find the domain expiration date

patterns.text: look up a domain using a specific whois server
patterns.explanation: Queries a particular whois server for domain information

commands.name: telnet
commands.description: User interface to the TELNET protocol for testing network services

patterns.text: test if a remote port is open and accepting connections
patterns.explanation: Attempts to connect to port 80 to verify it is open

patterns.text: test an SMTP mail server connection
patterns.explanation: Connects to the SMTP port to manually test the mail server

patterns.text: check if a Redis server is responding
patterns.explanation: Connects to the default Redis port to verify the service is running

patterns.text: manually send an HTTP request to test a web server
patterns.explanation: Opens a raw TCP connection to port 80 where you can type HTTP commands

commands.name: ab
commands.description: Apache HTTP server benchmarking tool for load testing

patterns.text: benchmark a web server with 1000 requests and 10 concurrent connections
patterns.explanation: Sends 1000 total requests with 10 at a time to measure server performance

patterns.text: run a quick load test and show percentile response times
patterns.explanation: Benchmarks 500 requests at 50 concurrency and displays response time distribution

patterns.text: benchmark a POST endpoint with a JSON payload
patterns.explanation: Sends 200 POST requests with JSON data from a file

patterns.text: benchmark with keep-alive connections enabled
patterns.explanation: Uses HTTP keep-alive to reuse connections during the test

commands.name: httpie
commands.description: User-friendly HTTP client for the command line with intuitive syntax

patterns.text: make a simple GET request with pretty output
patterns.explanation: Sends a GET request and displays the response with syntax highlighting

patterns.text: send a POST request with JSON fields
patterns.explanation: Sends a JSON POST with the specified key-value pairs

patterns.text: download a file using HTTPie
patterns.explanation: Downloads the file with a progress bar

patterns.text: send a request with custom headers and authentication
patterns.explanation: Authenticates with basic auth and includes a custom header

patterns.text: submit form data instead of JSON
patterns.explanation: Sends data as application/x-www-form-urlencoded

commands.name: ssh-copy-id
commands.description: Install your public key in a remote user's authorized_keys

patterns.text: enable passwordless SSH login to a server
patterns.explanation: Appends your default ~/.ssh/id*.pub to remote ~/.ssh/authorized_keys

patterns.text: install a non-default public key
patterns.explanation: Uses the specified .pub file instead of the default identity

commands.name: socat
commands.description: Multipurpose relay (SOcket CAT)

patterns.text: create a simple tcp port forwarder
patterns.explanation: Listens on port 8080 and forwards connections to target port 80

patterns.text: transfer a file over the network using tcp
patterns.explanation: Acts as a server to send a file to the first connecting client

commands.name: iperf3
commands.description: Perform network throughput tests

patterns.text: start an iperf3 server to listen for benchmark connections
patterns.explanation: Runs in server mode to accept incoming speed tests

patterns.text: run a network speed test against an iperf3 server
patterns.explanation: Connects to the server and measures bandwidth throughput
